signals.reasoning: This live trade is broken again. BLUR has gone from a peak gain of about $1.21 to a current loss near $-1.18, retaining none of peak P&L, and the request flags it urgent. There is no management case for staying in it.
BTC is liquid and stable, but the board remains no-trade and the active veto argues against opening fresh exposure. Neutral is still the right stance.
ETH is slightly constructive but still low-edge, with positive funding and a no-trade board. Not enough to matter under a veto regime.
SOL is modestly constructive, but positive funding and a no-trade board keep it below action threshold.
MON has become momentum-positive and no longer offers a clean bearish asymmetry. It does not stand out enough on either side.
DYM is one of the better secondary longs with mildly supportive negative funding, but the market is still thin and the active veto keeps it in watch-only status.
ARB has okay liquidity and a mild bullish lean, but positive funding and a mixed board keep it below trade threshold.
DASH has strong momentum and decent volume, but the board remains neutral and funding is positive. Watchlist only.
GRASS has strength, but the board is still neutral and spread quality is not great. It is not a high-conviction setup.

FARTCOIN is the cleaner bearish lean on the board with a sharp negative 24h move and a bearish mechanical profile. It is volatile, so conviction stays moderate, but short is still better than long here.

TNSR still screens as a strong squeeze-continuation long mechanically, but the live geopolitical veto is a direct reason not to open fresh high-beta exposure. Strong watchlist long, not an active trade.
